verdict.answer_first: A states the rule immediately: "never change the meaning of an existing contract; only add to it"; B opens with a generic setup before the principle.
verdict.decisiveness: A is more decisive: "default to URL path versioning" and "reserve major versions for breaking changes only"; B adds a "strong alternative" and more branches.
verdict.fabrication: B invents context: "This dir (`/private/tmp`) isn't a repo"; no such repo/path was given. A: none.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute: pick `/v1`, define breaking changes, run versions in parallel, publish deprecation policy, enforce with OpenAPI/CI.
